question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cups?
explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002).

question: Which athlete has won the most Olympic gold medals of all time?
explanation: Michael Phelps won 23 Olympic gold medals across four Olympic Games.

question: What sport is the Super Bowl associated with?
explanation: The Super Bowl is the championship game of the NFL.

question: In which city were the first modern Olympic Games held in 1896?
explanation: The first modern Olympics took place in Athens, Greece in 1896.

question: Which player has won the most Grand Slam tennis titles?
explanation: Novak Djokovic leads with 24 Grand Slam titles.

question: What is the maximum score in a single frame of bowling?
explanation: A perfect game in bowling is 300 — 12 consecutive strikes.

question: Lionel Messi has won the FIFA World Cup.
explanation: Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, with Messi winning the Golden Ball.

question: A regulation NBA basketball game has four 12-minute quarters.
explanation: NBA games consist of four 12-minute quarters (unlike FIBA's 10-minute quarters).

question: Usain Bolt won gold in the 100m at three consecutive Olympic Games.
explanation: Bolt won 100m gold at Beijing 2008, London 2012, and Rio 2016.

question: A marathon is exactly 26.2 miles long.
explanation: A marathon is 26.219 miles (42.195 km), a distance standardised after the 1908 London Olympics.

question: Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have both won the FIFA Ballon d'Or more than 5 times each.
explanation: Messi has won 8 Ballon d'Or awards; Ronaldo has won 5.

question: In boxing, a fight is stopped when the referee counts to ___.
explanation: A referee counts to ten (10) when a boxer is knocked down.

question: In tennis, a score of ___ means both players have won three points each.
explanation: Deuce occurs when both players are tied at 40-40. One player must then win two consecutive points to win the game.

question: In football (soccer), a player receives a ___ card for a serious foul and is sent off immediately.
explanation: A red card results in immediate dismissal from the game. Two yellow cards also result in a red.

question: The Olympic motto 'Citius, Altius, ___' means Faster, Higher, Stronger.
explanation: 'Citius, Altius, Fortius' (Faster, Higher, Stronger) has been the Olympic motto since 1894.

question: In American football, a ___ is worth six points.
explanation: A touchdown scores 6 points, followed by an opportunity for an extra point kick or 2-point conversion.

question: Rank these FIFA World Cup winning years chronologically (earliest first):
explanation: France 1998 → Italy 2006 → Germany 2014

question: Rank these sports by the number of players on each team (fewest first):
explanation: Tennis/singles (1) → Basketball (5) → American Football (11)

question: Rank these athletes by their 100m world record times (fastest first):
explanation: Bolt (9.58s) → Powell (9.72s) → Flo-Jo (10.49s — women's record)

question: Rank these Grand Slam tennis tournaments by the month they are held (earliest in the year first):
explanation: Australian Open (January) → Roland Garros (May/June) → Wimbledon (June/July)

question: Rank these football clubs by number of UEFA Champions League titles (fewest first):
explanation: Liverpool (6) → Barcelona (5) → Real Madrid (15) as of 2024

question: What is Usain Bolt's 100m world record time? (in seconds)
explanation: Usain Bolt set the world record of 9.58 seconds at the 2009 World Championships in Berlin.

question: How many goals did Lionel Messi score in the 2011/12 La Liga season?
explanation: Messi scored 50 league goals in 2011/12 — a La Liga record.

question: In what year were the first modern Olympic Games held?
explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens, Greece in 1896.

question: How many times has Brazil won the FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Brazil has won the World Cup 5 times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.

question: How long is a standard football (soccer) match in minutes?
explanation: A standard football match is 90 minutes — two halves of 45 minutes each, plus injury time.

question: How many players are on a standard football (soccer) team on the pitch?
explanation: Each team fields 11 players.

question: Which country hosts the Tour de France cycling race?
explanation: The Tour de France is a multi-stage road cycling race held primarily in France each July.

question: In which sport would you perform a slam dunk?
explanation: A slam dunk is a type of basketball shot where the player jumps and forces the ball down through the hoop.

question: Which country won the Rugby World Cup in 2023?
explanation: South Africa won the 2023 Rugby World Cup in France, their fourth World Cup title.

question: What colour is the jacket awarded to the Masters golf tournament winner?
explanation: The Masters Tournament awards the winner a distinctive green jacket, a tradition since 1949.

question: Which sport is played at Wimbledon?
explanation: Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world, held annually in London since 1877.

question: What nationality is Formula 1 driver Max Verstappen?
explanation: Max Verstappen is Dutch, born in Hasselt, Belgium to a Belgian mother and Dutch father.

question: In what country did the 2022 FIFA World Cup take place?
explanation: Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup — the first World Cup held in the Middle East.

question: What is the name of the trophy awarded to the NFL Super Bowl champions?
explanation: The Vince Lombardi Trophy is awarded to the Super Bowl winner, named after the legendary Green Bay Packers coach.

question: Which swimmer has won the most Olympic medals of all time?
explanation: Michael Phelps won 28 Olympic medals (23 gold) — the most of any Olympic athlete in history.

question: Who am I?
clues: I was born in 1985 in Rosario, Argentina and moved to Spain at age 13
I have won 8 Ballon d'Or awards — more than any other footballer in history
I spent the majority of my club career at FC Barcelona before moving to PSG and Inter Miami
I captained Argentina to victory at the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar

question: Who am I?
clues: I was born in Jamaica in 1986 and began my athletic career as a youth cricket player
I hold the world records in both the 100m and 200m sprint
I am the only person to win both the 100m and 200m at three consecutive Olympics
My nickname is 'Lightning Bolt' and my victory pose is iconic

question: Who am I?
clues: I retired from professional tennis in 2022 with more Grand Slam titles than anyone before me at the time
I am Swiss and known for my elegant one-handed backhand
I won Wimbledon a record 8 times
My famous rivalry with Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic defined a golden era of tennis

question: Who am I?
clues: I was born in Akron, Ohio in 1984 and was drafted first overall in the 2003 NBA Draft
I have won four NBA championships with three different teams
I am widely considered the greatest basketball player of all time
I am known as 'The King' and have played for the Cavaliers, Heat, and Lakers

question: Which sporting event am I?
clues: I am held every four years and was revived in Athens in 1896 after a 1,500-year hiatus
I include both Summer and Winter editions — each held in different years
My symbol is five interlocking rings representing the five inhabited continents
Athletes from over 200 nations compete under one flag of their country
